data.title: Buy Rating Reiterated on iBio as IBIO-610 Data Underscore Differentiated Efficacy; $5 Price Target Maintained
data.description:
Analyst Patrick Dolezal of LifeSci Capital maintained a Buy rating on Ibio, retaining the price target of $5.00.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Patrick Dolezal has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, starting with the exceptionally strong preclinical results for IBIO-610, which achieved nearly complete and durable activin E suppression in obese non-human primates after a single dose. He views this level of target engagement as highly differentiated versus competing INHBE-directed siRNA therapies, and believes it could translate into superior outcomes on weight loss and body composition measures in humans.

In addition, he highlights the encouraging combination data of IBIO-610 with semaglutide, where the regimen delivered greater reductions in visceral and total fat while meaningfully preserving lean mass compared with semaglutide alone. Coupled with prior translational evidence from other INHBE-targeting programs and a clear path toward an IND-equivalent filing for IBIO-610 in late 2026, he sees a compelling risk/reward profile that supports maintaining a Buy rating and a $5 price target.

Dolezal covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Neurogene, Oculis Holding, and MBX Biosciences, Inc.. According to TipRanks, Dolezal has an average return of 15.3% and a 42.86% success rate on recommended stocks.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Cantargia: Nadunolimab’s Complementary Role in PDAC Supports Maintained Buy Rating and Reaffirmed $14 Target
data.description:
In a report released today, Sara Nik from H.C. Wainwright reiterated a Buy rating on Cantargia AB, with a price target of SEK14.00.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Sara Nik has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to nadunolimab’s emerging role in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC). The recent KOL discussion underscored that nadunolimab, through IL1RAP targeting and stromal modulation, is positioned as a complementary agent to the new wave of RAS inhibitors rather than a direct rival, supporting durable relevance in a resistance-prone PDAC setting.

Additional planned trials further support the positive outlook, including a Phase 1b/2 study in metastatic PDAC combining nadunolimab with a RAS inhibitor and a neoadjuvant regimen pairing the drug with chemotherapy and a PD-1 inhibitor. Management and KOL feedback suggest the antibody could help delay or overcome resistance to RAS-pathway therapies, and with the price target reaffirmed at $14, Sara Nik views Cantargia AB’s risk-reward profile as attractive, justifying the maintained Buy recommendation.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: TeamViewer Kept at Hold as Analyst Cuts Price Target to €5.7 Amid Slow Growth and Execution Uncertainty
data.description:
Bernstein analyst Richard Nguyen reiterated a Hold rating on TeamViewer AG today and set a price target of €5.70.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Richard Nguyen has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors that balance TeamViewer’s strengths and weaknesses. He highlights that the company maintains robust profitability and healthy cash generation, yet shows only muted revenue expansion and limited signs that growth will meaningfully accelerate in the near term.

Nguyen also points out that the valuation now places more weight on EV/EBIT and less on long-dated DCF assumptions, reflecting higher uncertainty around the durability of growth and execution. While initiatives in Enterprise, the 1E integration, and early AI usage offer potential upside, they have not yet demonstrated scalable commercial impact, so he sees risk and reward as broadly offsetting each other, justifying a Hold stance and a reduced €5.7 price target.

data.title: Primaris REIT: Buy Rating Backed by Multi‑Year Growth Runway, Redevelopment Upside, and Strong Balance Sheet
data.description:
Tom Callaghan, an analyst from BMO Capital, maintained the Buy rating on Primaris Real Estate Investment Trust. The associated price target is C$25.00.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Tom Callaghan has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Primaris REIT’s strong year‑to‑date share performance and his view that the units still offer meaningful upside. He highlights a multi‑year growth runway supported by embedded leasing gains, particularly from recently signed and future leases, as well as the profitable re‑tenanting and redevelopment of former Hudson’s Bay (HBC) space, which is expected to drive solid net operating income and funds from operations growth.

Callaghan also points to Primaris’s robust balance sheet, disciplined capital recycling, and land monetization potential as key supports for the valuation and future returns. He notes that significant excess land and non‑core assets provide optionality for value realization, while a seasoned management team and ongoing operational execution underpin confidence in sustained same‑property NOI growth and further unit price appreciation, despite acknowledged leasing and tenant‑mix risks.

In another report released yesterday, TD Cowen also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a C$24.00 price target.

PMZ.UN’s price has also changed dramatically for the past six months – from C$15.570 to C$22.270, which is a 43.03% increase.

data.title: James Bales Maintains Buy Rating Despite Defence Digital Contract Loss, Cutting Price Target to A$21.30 While Highlighting Strong Upside and Resilient Growth
data.description:
James Bales, an analyst from Morgan Stanley, maintained the Buy rating on Objective Corporation Limited. The associated price target was lowered to A$21.30.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

James Bales has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, even after factoring in the Defence Digital Group contract non-renewal and the related downgrade of the price target to A$21.30 from its prior level. He appears to believe that, despite near-term pressure on FY26 annual recurring revenue and the A$12–17 million impact from the lost USP agreement, the core business model and long-term growth profile remain intact and compelling.

At the current share price of A$10.30, the implied upside to his revised target is still substantial, supporting a positive risk‑reward view on the stock. In addition, Bales likely views the company’s diversified customer base, resilient recurring revenue streams and margin profile as sufficient to absorb this contract setback while still delivering attractive returns for shareholders over time.

According to TipRanks, Bales is an analyst with an average return of -4.1% and a 36.25% success rate. Bales covers the Consumer Cyclical sector, focusing on stocks such as Flight Centre Travel Group Limited, WEB Travel Group, and Temple & Webster Group Ltd.
